task_completion.description: Create a Jupyter notebook named gravity_model.ipynb using the 'UrbanSim (Python 3)' kernel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Jupyter Notebook open with the filename 'gravity_model.ipynb' at the top, and the kernel indicator in the top right showing 'UrbanSim (Python 3)'.

task_completion.description: Link households and jobs to zones and calculate zone population and employment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for code cells reading 'sanfran_public.h5' and performing merges/groupby operations to sum 'persons' for population and count jobs for employment by zone.

task_completion.description: Calculate zone centroids and pairwise Euclidean distances.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for code calculating the mean x and y coordinates of parcels per zone, filtering for population > 0 and employment > 0, and computing a pairwise distance matrix (e.g., using scipy.spatial.distance or similar).

task_completion.description: Apply the gravity model formula and calculate zone interaction potential.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for code implementing the formula (Population_i * Employment_j) / Distance_ij^2.0 and summing these interactions to calculate Potential_i for each zone.

task_completion.description: Export the top 20 zone pairs by interaction strength to zone_interactions_top20.csv.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for code sorting the interactions descending, taking the top 20, and saving to '/home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/zone_interactions_top20.csv' with columns origin_zone, destination_zone, and interaction. Verify the file exists in the file explorer or terminal.

task_completion.description: Export zone interaction potential data to zone_interaction_potential.csv.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for code saving the potential data to '/home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/zone_interaction_potential.csv' with columns zone_id, population, employment, and interaction_potential, sorted descending. Verify the file exists in the file explorer or terminal.

task_completion.description: Save a heatmap visualization of the interaction matrix to interaction_heatmap.png.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for code generating a heatmap (e.g., using seaborn or matplotlib) and saving it to '/home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/interaction_heatmap.png'. The image file should be visible in the file explorer or an inline plot should be displayed in the notebook.
